notes: WIN-FND-002. Composition pin over the already-compiled theme-resolution routing chain from docs/design/01-foundations.md section 3: high contrast > dark > light precedence, the officecfg HighContrast override layer, the Windows platform HC (SPI_GETHIGHCONTRAST) and dark-mode (uxtheme.dll ordinal 132 ShouldAppsUseDarkMode) signal reads, and the WM_SETTINGCHANGE -> SalEvent::SettingsChanged -> Application::MergeSystemSettings app-wide re-resolution loop. This closes no source gap (the chain is already present and compiled); it locks control-flow ordering and platform-signal call sites against drift. Source evidence only: runtime_verified is false. The platform matrix (named Windows contrast themes, 100/125/200% scale, RTL/CJK), live-toggle interaction, and the settings-cascade performance budget stay the separate build/runtime gates and are not claimed here. The bounded accent scheme set is resolved through MaterialTokens::computeMaterialScheme (accent-scheme-composition checkpoint); Stage-1 adds only the scheme-key composition -- Violet stays the byte-identical default and no static token cache is re-keyed (Stage-3 live-apply).

checkpoints.note: updateSettings short-circuits on resolved high contrast: it sets mbHighContrast, restores the captured native baseline via updateNativeWidgetFrameworkSettings(nullptr), and returns false so no Material HC paint runs.

usesNativeFallback() keys off mbHighContrast alone (not a stale resolved mpDefinition); isNativeControlSupported dispatches to the native backend on it, so under HC every control routes to the platform theme.

UseDarkMode() layers the AUTO/LIGHT/DARK app colour mode; on AUTO it reads the undocumented uxtheme.dll ordinal 132 ShouldAppsUseDarkMode signal. Anchored on the ordinal literal + typedef so a prose reword cannot false-fail.

The generic global-settings pipeline applies the SAL_FORCE_HC env then the Accessibility::HighContrast officecfg override (0 Automatic / 1 Disable / 2 Enable), then captures the native baseline and invokes the Material bridge -- so the resolved HighContrastMode the Material overlay sees already reflects the override layer.

The file-definition widget draw backend is opt-in behind VCL_DRAW_WIDGETS_FROM_FILE; initWidgetDrawBackends only constructs the FileDefinitionWidgetDraw overlay when the flag is set (or a caller forces it).

The Windows frame reads the platform high-contrast flag via SPI_GETHIGHCONTRAST / HCF_HIGHCONTRASTON and feeds it into StyleSettings.SetHighContrastMode, upstream of the officecfg override and the Material bridge.
On WM_SETTINGCHANGE the frame refreshes dark mode (UpdateDarkMode) and marks the theme changed, then dispatches SalEvent::SettingsChanged -- the live half of a running-app contrast/colour switch.

ImplHandleSalSettings closes the loop app-wide on a SettingsChanged event: it re-merges system settings, lets the app override, and re-applies -- re-running the full resolution (which re-invokes the Material bridge per window).

MaterialTokens::computeMaterialScheme composes the active palette scheme key from the bounded accent base name + the dark flag: '<accent>' for light, '<accent>-dark' for dark. Violet is the unnamed default so an empty accent base yields '' (light) / 'dark' (dark), keeping the pre-accent resolution path byte-identical. fromThemeDefinition(std::string_view, bool) resolves through computeMaterialScheme. This is orthogonal to the HC>dark>light precedence above; Stage-1 adds only the scheme-key composition and re-keys no static token cache (the live-apply re-key is separate Stage-3 work), so genuine default captures never drift.

contiguous.note: The HC short-circuit must set the flag, restore the native baseline, and return false without any Material paint, in that exact order -- a reordered or comment-only bypass fails closed here even though the individual markers still resolve.
contiguous.statement: if (rSettings.GetStyleSettings().GetHighContrastMode()) { { std::scoped_lock aGuard(m_pThemeState->maMutex); m_pThemeState->mbHighContrast = true; } updateNativeWidgetFrameworkSettings(nullptr); return false; }

contiguous.note: The native StyleSettings baseline must be captured before the Material overlay runs, so resolved high contrast can restore genuine platform colours: capture precedes update as a contiguous pair.
The app-wide re-resolution must merge system settings, apply the app override, then set the settings, in that order.
contiguous.statement: Application::MergeSystemSettings( aSettings ); pApp->OverrideSystemSettings( aSettings ); Application::SetSettings( aSettings );
